# Regression — what could the recent code break?

**Verdict:** ship-with-caution — every load-bearing invariant of the Jun 12–13 fix arc (`9edb92c~1..3fbc39a`) survived adversarial verification; the residue is a small set of confirmed LOW-severity regressions/gaps, each with a cheap fix and none reaching the security or a11y-guarantee boundary.

> **✅ Remediation (2026-06-15):** all five LOW items fixed + tested. Gradient stop-alpha
> now composited over the opaque base (`run-contrast.ts`; translucent-dark-stop test
> BLOCKs); `importCanvas` provenance write is best-effort (a DB error no longer discards
> a completed import); router routes negated-a11y (`'inaccessible'`) → remediate; the
> C11 `doneReason==='length'` truncation is propagated through the orchestrator and a
> truncated draft is flagged incomplete in `view.text`; audit-shell contrast regression
> test added (styled link/button/th/blockquote through the real auditor). See
> [SHIP-READINESS.md](./SHIP-READINESS.md#-remediation-status-2026-06-15--all-8-conditions-addressed).

## Summary

This pillar is healthy. The arc's structural guarantees are intact and code-confirmed: the output gate still blocks both `blocker` and `error` and withholds the badge (`gate.ts:68`), the gate enforcement locus in `createAppApi` is byte-for-byte unchanged so a router misroute cannot bypass it (`app-api.ts:367-375,460-464`), the frozen cross-track contract (`src/contracts/index.ts`) was not touched, and the C13 (iterative repair) and C15 (whole-hierarchy heading demotion) rewrites preserve output semantics. The genuine regressions introduced by the arc are all low severity: a router misroute on negated-a11y phrasing, a DB-write coupling that can discard a completed read-only import, a narrow new gradient fail-open from dropped stop alpha, and a still-unenforced truncated-draft signal (C11). Under the single-user on-device threat model none of these are ship-blockers, but four carry trivial/cheap fixes worth landing.

## Confirmed findings

Ordered by threat-model-adjusted severity (no critical/high/medium findings — the arc introduced no security or a11y-guarantee regression).

### LOW

| Title | Location | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|
| Router misroutes negated-a11y audit requests ('is my page inaccessible') to BUILD instead of remediate | `src/orchestrator/router.ts:92-94,124-134` | `countStemHits` compiles `\b${needle}`, so `\baccessib` cannot match inside "inaccessible"; weakHits=0, then the bare noun matches BUILD_NOUNS at step 5 → build. Add 'inaccess' to REMEDIATION_WEAK or relax the 'accessib' boundary to substring; test both 'is my page inaccessible' and 'this syllabus is inaccessible' → remediate. UX-only; gate runs in either mode. |
| importCanvas couples a completed read-only import to a DB provenance write — a DB error discards the import | `src/runtime/app-api.ts:471-495` | Arc changed `return importer(...)` to: resolve `result`, then `await db.run(INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ...)`, then `return result`. If `database()`/`db.run` rejects (locked DB, disk full, migration error), the completed import is lost (IPC turns the rejection into an error envelope). Wrap the INSERT in try/catch, log-and-continue, return `result` regardless; test that importCanvas still resolves when db.run rejects. Recoverable via idempotent re-import, but a real robustness regression. |
| New gradient adjudicator can fail-OPEN on a translucent/transparent dark stop | `src/engine/render/run-contrast.ts:54-78,105-114`; `src/engine/contrast.ts:100-135` | Verified: `gradientCandidates` calls `parseColor` per stop, which drops alpha (`parseRgb` returns only `{r,g,b}`), and the gradient case (105-114) scores `worstAgainst` directly **without** compositing over the base — unlike the layers case (90-103) which does composite. So `linear-gradient(rgba(0,0,0,0.5), rgba(0,0,0,0))` becomes opaque-black candidates; light text scores ~21:1 and PASSES while the real composited background would fail. New to this arc (pre-arc ignored gradients entirely). Composite each stop's alpha over the underlying base before scoring; add the translucent-dark-stop test. |
| doneReason='length' (C11) is detectable on the transport but no production path consumes it to block a truncated draft | `src/llm/client.ts:81-84,111-114`; `src/llm/types.ts` (only test consumers) | Detection wired, enforcement not: `grep -r doneReason src/` shows only types/client/tests read it; the orchestrator rebuilds a fresh ChatResult that drops `chunk.doneReason`. Invariant #3 ("truncated draft must be detectable AND never surfaced as final") is half-met. Wire a consumer at turn assembly to flag `doneReason==='length'` as incomplete. Mitigated: drafts are labeled unverified, the HTML gate is independent, and a truncated structured ChangeLog usually fails JSON.parse → error. Additive incomplete-wiring, not an arc regression. |
| Text over a background image now WARNS (non-blocking) instead of being silently checked against the solid behind it; below-fold image runs defer to manual-review alert | `src/engine/render/run-contrast.ts:115-123`; `auditor.ts:39`; `playwright-runner.ts:128-186`; `gate.ts:62-86` | Materially better detection than pre-arc (which walked past the image to the nearest solid, defaulting white — a silent fail-open). Residuals are deliberate severity-by-certainty design and both surfaced: failing image-backed text → `conformance.warnings`; below-fold (box at y≥900 → `clampClip` returns null → `unresolvable`) → `needsHumanReview`. Confirm calibration with product; raise RENDER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT / use a full-page screenshot, or document the 900px fold limit. |

### INFO (verified-safe behavior changes and intact-invariant confirmations — no defect)

|---|---|---|

Depth-20000 round-trip + nested post-order tests pass (33/33). |
| C15 fix (whole-hierarchy heading demotion) intact — intentional verdict change | `src/engine/allowlist.ts:510-520,621,153-156` | `shiftHeadings` is computed document-globally from the flat token stream; h1+h3 now emits h2+h4 (gap preserved) vs old h2+h3. Confirmed no stale golden fixtures elsewhere assumed the old h1-only rule. (Minor: the heading-shift branch is at 510-520, not 507-519.) |

Both err toward caution. The one exception is the gradient alpha-drop fail-open captured as LOW above. |
| Gate still blocks blocker+error and withholds the badge (C2/C12 healthy) | `src/orchestrator/gate.ts:68-92` | `BLOCKING = {'blocker','error'}` identical at `9edb92c~1`; the only diff is line 80's tag-scoped removed-semantic id. `badgeWithheld = blockers.length>0` unchanged. Arch invariant #2 holds. |
| Per-tag removed-semantic id is a correctness fix with no downstream breakage | `src/orchestrator/gate.ts:79-81`; `app-api.ts:431-436` | Scoped id `allowlist-removed-semantic:${tag}`; all consumers match via `.startsWith(...)`, so no hard-coded old string breaks. Correctly makes each removed tag a distinct tracked blocker for the before/after diff (uniqueById keys on id). |
| Bare artifact-noun + weak-a11y (no verb) now routes to remediate — intended L5b tradeoff | `src/orchestrator/router.ts:62-67,124-134` | REMEDIATION_WEAK (step 4) checked before BUILD_NOUNS (step 5), so 'an accessible quiz' → remediate; verb-present 'create an accessible page' still → build. Documented in router header + L5b test. Gate runs in either mode. |
| Gate enforcement locus unchanged — a router misroute cannot bypass the gate | `src/runtime/app-api.ts:367-375,411-429,459-464` | `runStandardTurn` loops every fragment through `enforceGate` with no mode conditional; `runRemediate` gates before/after/each re-audit. `git diff` shows the sole arc change to this file is the canvas_imports UPSERT. Arch invariant #2 preserved. |
| Canvas-shell unification correctly CLOSES the preview/audit parity gap | `src/engine/render/canvas-shell.ts:23-55`; `preview.ts:22-24`; `playwright-runner.ts:157` | Deleted preview `CANVAS_CSS` is byte-identical to `CANVAS_SHELL_CSS`; the old audit shell carried only animation+body rules, so the audit GAINED element styling. Both sides now consume one function. (Minor: the parity test at `canvas-shell.test.ts:22` only directly guards the preview side; the audit path has no pinning test.) |
| Audit side now applies link/button/th/blockquote colors it never applied before — verified-safe | `src/engine/render/canvas-shell.ts:30,35,36,38`; `auditor.ts:61-63` | Recomputed default pairs: link/button 5.04:1, blockquote 6.37:1, th 10.57:1 — all pass AA, so no live regression. A brand-kit override nudging these darker could now correctly flip to a blocker. Genuinely missing: a regression test running a styled link+button+th+blockquote through the real auditor. |
| Frozen contract untouched; the breaking type change is render-internal and fully migrated | `src/contracts/index.ts` (unchanged); `src/engine/render/types.ts:42-72`; `render/index.ts:32-35` | `git log 9edb92c~1..3fbc39a -- src/contracts/index.ts` is empty; `Auditor` returns `IssueSet` (decoupled from `ScanResult`). The `ScanResult.textRuns` break has no external consumers; only live token is an explanatory comment. |
| client.ts doneReason/error (C11) additions do NOT regress tool-call surfacing | `src/llm/client.ts:69-118`; `client.test.ts` | The C11 diff is purely transport-additive; tool-call extraction lines are byte-identical context. Error guards throw only on a standalone non-empty error string (no content/tool_calls). All 5 client tests pass including the C1 transport test. |

## Checked & cleared / refuted

- **REFUTED — Gradient adjudicator badging a gradient that dips below AA between the ~10% grid samples** (`run-contrast.ts:53-78`). The grid-sampling aliasing is a real abstract property but **unreachable at the shipped config**. With `gradientSamples=9` (10% steps), the WCAG AA contrast gap required for two flanking samples to straddle a crossing while both pass (≥~0.40 large / ≥~0.96 normal text) far exceeds the maximum sRGB luminance change in one 10% step (~0.213, at the white end of a black→white pair). `gradientSamples` is an app-set constant, never attacker-controlled (the malicious-gradient vector only picks stop colors), so the miss-window is mathematically closed. Non-issue. *(Note: the distinct, real gradient fail-open is the dropped-alpha path captured as a LOW finding — different mechanism.)*
- **Verified intact, not regressions** (so the reader trusts coverage): the unconditional server-side gate (`gate.ts:68`, blocking set identical to base), the gate enforcement locus (`app-api.ts` — only arc change is the provenance UPSERT), the frozen contract (`src/contracts/index.ts` — empty arc diff), C13/C15 repair output semantics, the per-tag removed-semantic id (no hard-coded-string consumer), the Canvas-shell CSS byte-identity, and C1 tool-call surfacing across the C11 transport additions. All confirmed against both current source and `git diff`/`git show` of the arc.
- **Out of scope but worth noting** the synthesis did not re-litigate: the known-open `view.text` conformance-claim-in-prose injection (the gate guards the badge/HTML, not free prose) and the LLM daemon-respawn/mutex-timeout resilience gaps — these are pre-existing deferred items, not arc regressions.

## Action items before ship

Prioritized by value/effort. None block ship under the threat model; the first four are cheap correctness/robustness wins.

1. **[cheap] Gradient alpha fail-open** — composite each gradient stop's alpha over the underlying opaque base in `run-contrast.ts` (mirror the layers case at 90-103) before calling `worstAgainst`. Test: light text over `linear-gradient(rgba(0,0,0,0.5), rgba(0,0,0,0))` over a dark page → assert it BLOCKS (not ~21:1 pass). This is the only confirmed fail-open in the arc, even if narrow.
2. **[cheap] importCanvas best-effort provenance** — wrap the `db.run` INSERT (`app-api.ts:477-493`) in try/catch, log-and-continue, return `result` regardless. Test: inject a db whose `run()` rejects; assert importCanvas still resolves to the importer result.
3. **[trivial] Router negated-a11y routing** — add 'inaccess' to REMEDIATION_WEAK (or relax the 'accessib' stem to substring while keeping word-boundary for 'fix'). Tests: `routeIntent('is my page inaccessible').mode === 'remediate'` and `'this syllabus is inaccessible' === 'remediate'`; keep the L5b 'is my syllabus accessible' → remediate guard.
4. **[medium] Wire the C11 truncated-draft consumer** — at orchestrator turn assembly, propagate and act on `doneReason==='length'` so a num_predict-truncated alt-text/structured draft is flagged incomplete and never surfaced as final. Test: a turn whose LLM result has `doneReason='length'` is not presented as a final/verified draft.
5. **[cheap] Audit-shell contrast regression test** — run a fragment containing a styled link, button, `<th>`, and blockquote through the REAL `createAuditor(playwrightRunner)` and assert zero spurious contrast blockers for the default Canvas shell colors, guarding future shell-color edits. (The lone clean-fragment integration test uses only h2/p and is skipped unless `RUN_BROWSER_INTEGRATION=1`.)
6. **[product + optional medium] Image-contrast calibration** — confirm the severity-by-certainty design (deterministic blocks; image worst-case warns; below-fold alerts) with product. Either raise `RENDER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT` / use a full-page screenshot so below-fold image text is adjudicated, or document the 900px fold limitation. Integration test: white text over a dark background-image taller than the viewport → lower run yields an `image`/`unresolvable` issue and the failing run is a `warning` surfaced in `conformance.warnings`.
